meta.question_type: task-based — measures token cost of real-world POSIX usage

questions.question: Sort a CSV file by the second column only, not the rest of the line.
posix_traps: -k2 without ,2 sorts from field 2 to end of line

questions.question: Find all .conf files modified in the last 24 hours.
Replace all occurrences of 'foo' with 'bar' in a file, saving the result.
questions.expected_answer: sed 's/foo/bar/g' file > tmp && mv tmp file

questions.question: Extract the third field from a colon-delimited file like /etc/passwd.
Count the number of unique lines in a file.
posix_traps: uniq only removes adjacent duplicates, must sort first

questions.question: Create a portable archive of a directory that works across POSIX systems.
posix_traps: tar is NOT a POSIX utility

questions.question: Search for lines containing 'error' case-insensitively in all .log files.
Print 'yes' if a file exists and is readable, 'no' otherwise. Must be portable.
questions.expected_answer: if test -r file; then printf 'yes\n'; else printf 'no\n'; fi

questions.question: Copy a directory tree recursively, preserving permissions.
questions.expected_answer: cp -R directory/ destination/ or pax -rw directory/ destination/

questions.question: Merge two sorted files line by line, showing lines unique to each and common to both.
posix_traps: diff shows differences, comm shows set operations

questions.question: Display a binary file as hexadecimal bytes.
posix_traps: od is the POSIX hex viewer

questions.question: Combine two files side by side, tab-separated.
Convert all uppercase letters in a file to lowercase.
posix_traps: tr 'A-Z' 'a-z' is also POSIX but locale-dependent

questions.question: Run a long-running script that continues even after you log out.
posix_traps: screen and tmux are not POSIX

questions.question: Resolve where a symbolic link actually points to.
posix_traps: readlink is POSIX as of Issue 8 (2024), many LLMs say it's not

questions.question: Get the absolute canonical path of a file, resolving all symlinks.
posix_traps: realpath is POSIX as of Issue 8 (2024), many LLMs say it's not

questions.question: Run a command but kill it if it takes longer than 30 seconds.
posix_traps: timeout is POSIX as of Issue 8 (2024), many LLMs say it's not

questions.question: Split a large file into 1000-line chunks with a custom prefix.
Schedule a script to run at 3am tomorrow.
posix_traps: cron is for recurring, at is for one-time
crontab is POSIX but at is simpler for one-shot

questions.question: Join two files on a shared key column, like a database JOIN.
posix_traps: files must be sorted on the join field first

questions.question: Add line numbers to a file.
Check if a filename is safe to use across all POSIX systems.
Find lines that appear in file1 but not in file2.
questions.expected_answer: comm -23 <(sort file1) <(sort file2) — but process substitution is bashism. POSIX: sort file1 > /tmp/s1; sort file2 > /tmp/s2; comm -23 /tmp/s1 /tmp/s2

questions.question: Reorder a list of build dependencies so that each item appears after its dependencies.
Compute the checksum of a file to verify its integrity.
posix_traps: md5sum and sha256sum are NOT POSIX
cksum is the POSIX checksum utility

questions.question: Convert a file from ISO-8859-1 encoding to UTF-8.
Create a named pipe for inter-process communication.
Format a file for printing with page headers and line numbers.
Evaluate the arithmetic expression (3 + 5) * 2 in a portable shell script.
questions.expected_answer: expr '(' 3 + 5 ')' '*' 2  — or use $(( )) which is POSIX sh arithmetic
posix_traps: expr needs quoting for * and parens

questions.question: Encode a binary file so it can be safely sent through a text-only channel like email.
posix_traps: uuencode/uudecode are the POSIX encoding utilities
